src.osir_lib.osir_lib.core.FileManager module

class src.osir_lib.osir_lib.core.FileManager.FileManager[source]

Bases: object

static create_case(directory: str, case_name: str)[source]

Create a new case directory

Parameters:

  • directory (str) – The directory where the case will be created.

  • case_name (str) – The name of the case (will be the directory name).

Returns:

The path to the created case directory.

Return type:

str

Raises:

FileExistsError – If the case directory already exists.

static get_cases(directory)[source]

Get a list of subdirectories in a given directory.

Parameters:

directory (str) – The directory to search.

Returns:

A list of subdirectory names.

Return type:

list[str]

static get_cases_path(case_name: str) → Path | None[source]
static get_config_path(config: str) → Path[source]
static get_files_in_cases(directory)[source]

Get a list of files in a given directory (recursive).

Parameters:

directory (str) – The directory to search for files.

Returns:

A list of relative file paths found in the directory.

Return type:

list[str]

static get_module_path(module: str) → Path[source]
static get_profile_path(profile: str, raise_error: bool = True) → Path[source]
static get_yaml_files(directory, relative=False)[source]

Get a list of .yml files in a given directory recursively.

Parameters:

  • directory (str) – The directory to search for .yml files.

  • relative (bool) – If True, return paths relative to directory. If False, return basenames.

Returns:

A list of YAML file paths or basenames.

Return type:

list[str]

static load_yaml_file(filepath)[source]

Load and parse content from a YAML file.

Parameters:

filepath (str) – The path to the YAML file.

Returns:

The parsed YAML content.

Return type:

dict

static resolve_modules_parent_dir(modules)[source]

Convert a list of module filenames to their relative paths from the base modules directory.

Parameters:

modules (list[str]) – A list of module file names (e.g., “foo.yml”).

Returns:

Relative paths under the modules directory (e.g., “windows/foo.yml”).

Return type:

list[str]

src.osir_lib.osir_lib.core.LogUtils module

class src.osir_lib.osir_lib.core.LogUtils.LogUtils(ctx: OsirModule)[source]

Bases: object

A utility class for processing Unix-based log files, handling log writing, reading, and formatting.

case_path

Path to the directory where case-related files are stored.

Type:

str

module

Instance of the module being used for processing.

Type:

OsirModule

default_output_dir

Default directory path for storing output logs based on the module name.

Type:

str

__init__(ctx: OsirModule)[source]
static date_format(log_line)[source]

Determines the date format for a given log line by matching against known patterns.

Parameters:

log_line (str) – The log line to analyze for date format.

Returns:

The matching date regex pattern or logs a warning if no format is found.

Return type:

str

static get_date(line, regex=None, strtime=None)[source]

Parses a date from a log line based on a given regex or inferred format.

Parameters:

  • line (str) – The log line to parse for date information.

  • regex (Optional[str]) – Regular expression pattern for date extraction.

  • strtime (Optional[str]) – Date format string for parsing.

Returns:

ISO formatted date string or “N/A” if no date found.

Return type:

str

get_log()[source]

Generates each line from a log file, handling .gz compressed files if needed.

Test multiple encodings :

‘utf-8’, ‘latin1’, ‘iso-8859-1’, ‘windows-1252’

Yields:

str – A single log line.

static get_severity(line)[source]

Extracts the severity level from a log line based on predefined keywords.

Parameters:

line (str) – The log line from which to extract the severity.

Returns:

The extracted severity level in lowercase, or “N/A” if not found.

Return type:

str

safe_search(pattern: str, log: str) → str[source]

Searches for a regex pattern in a log line and returns the first group if found.

Parameters:

  • pattern (str) – Regex pattern to search for.

  • log (str) – The log line to search within.

Returns:

First matching group, or None if no match is found.

Return type:

str

start_writer_thread(output_path=None)[source]

Starts a writer thread that will handle saving data to JSONL files.

Returns:

A queue that will hold data to be processed by the writer thread.

Return type:

Queue

src.osir_lib.osir_lib.core.OsirAgentConfig module

class src.osir_lib.osir_lib.core.OsirAgentConfig.BoxDetails(*, host: str, user: str, password: str, custom_mountpoint: str = 'C')[source]

Bases: BaseModel

Defines the connection parameters for a remote Windows forensic environment.

This model stores the credentials and host information required for the agent to mount and interact with a remote Windows machine where forensic tools might be executed.

Parameters:

  • host (str) – IP address or hostname of the remote Windows box.

  • user (str) – Username for authentication.

  • password (str) – Password for authentication.

  • custom_mountpoint (str) – The drive letter or path (default ‘C’) to be used during tasks.

custom_mountpoint: str
host: str
model_config = {}

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

password: str
user: str
class src.osir_lib.osir_lib.core.OsirAgentConfig.FullAgentConfig(*, master: MasterConfig, windows_box: WindowsBoxConfig, splunk: SplunkConfig)[source]

Bases: BaseModel

The root validation model for the ‘agent.yml’ configuration file.

It acts as a single point of truth, validating that the Master, Windows Box, and Splunk sections are correctly formatted and present before the agent starts.

master: MasterConfig
model_config = {}

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

splunk: SplunkConfig
windows_box: WindowsBoxConfig
class src.osir_lib.osir_lib.core.OsirAgentConfig.MasterConfig(*, host: str)[source]

Bases: BaseModel

Specifies the network coordinates of the OSIR Master node.y

Parameters:

host (str) – The IP address or DNS name of the OSIR Master.

host: str
model_config = {}

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

class src.osir_lib.osir_lib.core.OsirAgentConfig.SplunkConfig(*, host: str, user: str, password: str, port: int, mport: int, ssl: bool)[source]

Bases: BaseModel

Contains the authentication and connection parameters for Splunk integration.

Parameters:

  • host (str) – The address of the Splunk server.

  • user (str) – Username for the Splunk.

  • password (str) – Password for the Splunk.

  • port (int) – The destination port for data ingestion (usually HEC or forwarder).

  • mport (int) – The Splunk Management Port (default 8089).

  • ssl (bool) – Whether to use encrypted HTTPS for the connection.

host: str
model_config = {}

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

mport: int
password: str
port: int
ssl: bool
user: str
class src.osir_lib.osir_lib.core.OsirAgentConfig.WindowsBoxConfig(*, location: str, cores: int | None = None, remote_box: BoxDetails)[source]

Bases: BaseModel

Defines the execution environment for Windows-based forensic modules.

In the OSIR workflow, ‘location’ determines if the tools run locally, in a Docker container (dockur), or on a remote machine. This model also allows for resource allocation by specifying CPU cores, ensuring that heavy forensic parsing doesn’t overwhelm the host system.

Parameters:

  • location (str) – The deployment type (e.g., ‘local’, ‘remote’, ‘dockur’).

  • cores (int, optional) – The number of CPU cores allocated to the forensic tasks.

  • remote_box (BoxDetails) – Nested connection details for remote execution.

cores: int | None
location: str
model_config = {}

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

remote_box: BoxDetails

src.osir_lib.osir_lib.core.OsirConnector module

class src.osir_lib.osir_lib.core.OsirConnector.OsirConnector(*, json2splunk: list[Json2SplunkConfigModel] | None = None, elastic: list[Json2ElasticModel] | None = None)[source]

Bases: OsirConnectorModel

model_config = {}

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

src.osir_lib.osir_lib.core.OsirConstants module

src.osir_lib.osir_lib.core.OsirDecorator module

src.osir_lib.osir_lib.core.OsirDecorator.finalize_task(status, task_id, func_name, start, end, logs)[source]

Updates the PostgreSQL database with the final results and logs of a task.

Parameters:

  • status (ProcessingStatus) – The final status (DONE or FAILED).

  • task_id (int) – The unique ID of the task in the database.

  • func_name (str) – The name of the module/function that executed.

  • start (datetime) – Execution start timestamp.

  • end (datetime) – Execution end timestamp.

  • logs (str) – The raw log output captured during execution.

src.osir_lib.osir_lib.core.OsirDecorator.osir_internal_module(cls_or_func=None, *, trace: bool | None = True)[source]

Core OSIR decorator for internal modules that automates task state and logging.

Parameters:

  • cls_or_func (Any) – The class or function representing the internal module.

  • trace (bool) – Whether to capture and persist logs to the database.

Returns:

The decorated module ready for the OSIR task dispatcher.

Return type:

Callable

src.osir_lib.osir_lib.core.OsirDecorator.pinfo(func)[source]

Decorator to log the lifecycle of file processing within an OSIR module.

Parameters:

func (Callable) – The processing function to be wrapped.

Returns:

The wrapped function with start/end logging for the input file.

Return type:

Callable

src.osir_lib.osir_lib.core.OsirDecorator.timeit(func)[source]

Decorator that measures and logs the execution time of a specific function.

Parameters:

func (Callable) – The function whose execution time is to be measured.

Returns:

The wrapped function with performance logging enabled.

Return type:

Callable

src.osir_lib.osir_lib.core.OsirDecorator.trace_func()[source]

Decorator used to capture function output and update the task status in the DB.

Returns:

The decorated function with full database tracing.

Return type:

Callable

src.osir_lib.osir_lib.core.OsirInput module

class src.osir_lib.osir_lib.core.OsirInput.OsirInput(*, type: Literal['file', 'dir'], path: str | None = None, name: str | None = None, file: str | None = None, dir: str | None = None, paths: list[str] | None = None, match: Path, match_updated: Path | None = None, updated: bool | None = False)[source]

Bases: OsirInputModel, OsirPathTransformerMixin

Manages the input data source for an OSIR module, handling path resolution and transformation.

_context

Private reference to the parent module context.

Type:

OsirModule

match

The original match file path.

Type:

Path

match_updated

The resolved path after transformations are applied.

Type:

Optional[Path]

get_input_name_safe() → str[source]

Returns a truncated and filesystem-safe version of the input filename.

Returns:

The sanitized filename string.

Return type:

str

match: Path
match_updated: Path | None
model_config = {'arbitrary_types_allowed': True}

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

model_post_init(context: Any, /) → None

This function is meant to behave like a BaseModel method to initialise private attributes.

It takes context as an argument since that’s what pydantic-core passes when calling it.

Parameters:

  • self – The BaseModel instance.

  • context – The context.

update() → OsirInput[source]

Applies path transformation logic to the input match.

Returns:

The current instance with updated path information.

Return type:

OsirInput

updated: bool | None

src.osir_lib.osir_lib.core.OsirModule module

class src.osir_lib.osir_lib.core.OsirModule.OsirModule(*, version: float | str, author: str, module: str, description: str, os: Literal['windows', 'unix', 'ios', 'macos', 'network', 'android', 'generic'], type: Literal['pre-process', 'process', 'post-process', 'post_parsing'], disk_only: bool, no_multithread: bool, processor_type: list[Literal['internal', 'external']], processor_os: Literal['windows', 'unix'], optional: dict | None = None, alt_module: str | None = None, env: list[str] | None = None, tool: OsirTool | None = None, input: OsirInput | None = None, output: OsirOutput | None = None, endpoint: Pattern | None = None, connector: OsirConnector | None = None, splunk: dict | None = None, filename: str | None = None, case_path: Path = None, endpoint_name: str | None = None)[source]

Bases: OsirModuleModel

Main domain class representing a forensic processing module within OSIR.

case_path

The filesystem path to the current forensic case.

Type:

Path

tool

The forensic tool/binary logic associated with this module.

Type:

OsirTool

input

The source file or data to be processed.

Type:

OsirInput

output

The destination and formatting logic for results.

Type:

OsirOutput

endpoint_name

The name of the workstation or source of the artifact.

Type:

str

__init__(**data)[source]

Initializes the OsirModule by converting base models module into OsirModule.

property case_name: str

Extracts the identifier of the current case from the filesystem path.

Returns:

The lowercase name of the case directory.

Return type:

str

case_path: Path
connector: OsirConnector | None
endpoint_name: str | None
input: OsirInput | None
property is_wsl

Runtime detection for the Windows Subsystem for Linux environment.

Returns:

True if executing inside WSL, False otherwise.

Return type:

bool

link_and_update() → OsirModule[source]

Post-initialization validator that establishes component relationships.

The fully linked and updated module instance.

Return type:

OsirModule

This function is meant to behave like a BaseModel method to initialise private attributes.

output: OsirOutput | None

Constructs the absolute path where the module results will be stored.

The joined path as a string, or empty if no case_path is set.

str

tool: OsirTool | None

Ensures internal Python-based modules are discoverable and valid.

Raises:

ValueError – If the internal module logic cannot be loaded.

src.osir_lib.osir_lib.core.OsirOutput module

class src.osir_lib.osir_lib.core.OsirOutput.OsirOutput(*, type: str | None = None, format: str | None = None, output_dir: str | None = None, output_file: str | None = None, output_prefix: str | None = None, output_prefix_no_endpoint: str | None = None, updated: bool | None = False)[source]

Bases: OsirOutputModel, OsirPathTransformerMixin

Manages the generation, naming, and structural organization of forensic tool outputs.

_context

Private reference to the parent module for accessing shared case metadata.

Type:

OsirModule

output_prefix_no_endpoint

Internal storage for the naming prefix excluding the endpoint name, used for regex matching during renames.

Type:

str

updated

Flag to ensure path transformations are only performed once.

Type:

bool

src.osir_lib.osir_lib.core.OsirPathTransformerMixin module

class src.osir_lib.osir_lib.core.OsirPathTransformerMixin.OsirPathTransformerMixin[source]

Bases: object

Mixin centralizing the path transformation logic for the OSIR framework.

apply_suffix(field_name: str, return_value: bool = False)[source]

Retrieves a specific field’s value, transforms it, and reassigns it.

Parameters:

  • field_name (str) – The name of the attribute to transform (e.g., ‘match’, ‘output_dir’).

  • return_value (bool) – If True, returns the path instead of setting the attribute.

static safe_format(text: str, **kwargs) → str[source]

Performs placeholder substitution in strings using direct replacement.

Parameters:

  • text (str) – The template string (e.g., “results_{endpoint_name}.csv”).

  • **kwargs – Key-value pairs for substitution.

Returns:

The formatted string with placeholders replaced.

Return type:

str

update()[source]

Abstract method intended to be implemented by child models.

Represents a forensic execution profile within the OSIR framework.

A list of fully initialized OsirModule objects derived from the profile configuration.

src.osir_lib.osir_lib.core.OsirSingleton module

src.osir_lib.osir_lib.core.OsirSingleton.singleton(cls)[source]

Decorator to transform a class into a singleton. Ensures that only one instance of the class exists within the application.

Parameters:

cls (type) – The class to be transformed into a singleton.

Returns:

A wrapper function that manages the instantiation of the singleton class, ensuring only one instance is created.

Return type:

function

src.osir_lib.osir_lib.core.OsirTool module

class src.osir_lib.osir_lib.core.OsirTool.OsirTool(*, path: str, cmd: str, source: str | None = None, version: str | float | None = None, license: str | None = None, env: list[str] | None = None)[source]

Bases: OsirToolModel, OsirPathTransformerMixin

Orchestrates the execution of forensic binaries across varied environments.

This class acts as the final translation layer in the OSIR pipeline. It takes the high-level tool definitions and adapts them—via path transformations and command formatting—to run correctly on Linux (Local), Windows (Remote WinRM), or Windows Subsystem for Linux (WSL).

_context

Private link to the parent module providing metadata.

Type:

OsirModule

path

The resolved filesystem path to the tool binary.

Type:

str

cmd

The fully formatted command-line string with all arguments.

Type:

str

__init__(**data)[source]

Create a new model by parsing and validating input data from keyword arguments.

Raises [ValidationError][pydantic_core.ValidationError] if the input data cannot be validated to form a valid model.

self is explicitly positional-only to allow self as a field name.

init_tool(processor_os)[source]

Validates the existence of the tool binary on the local or remote filesystem.

Raises:

FileNotFoundError – If the binary cannot be located in any known path.

model_config = {}

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

model_post_init(context: Any, /) → None

This function is meant to behave like a BaseModel method to initialise private attributes.

It takes context as an argument since that’s what pydantic-core passes when calling it.

Parameters:

  • self – The BaseModel instance.

  • context – The context.

run(**kwargs) → bool[source]

Primary entry point to start the tool execution.

Returns:

True if the execution completed without unhandled exceptions.

Return type:

bool

run_local(is_agent=False, timeout=3600) → bool[source]

Spawns a local subprocess to run the tool directly on the current host.

Returns:

The captured stdout and stderr of the process.

Return type:

Tuple[str, str]

run_remote()[source]

Executes the command on a remote Windows host using the WinRM protocol.

Returns:

Success status and the combined output logs.

Return type:

Tuple[bool, str]

run_wsl(timeout=3600)[source]

Executes a Windows binary from within a Linux WSL environment.

Returns:

The command output logs.

Return type:

Tuple[str, str]

run_wsl_bak()[source]

Executes a Windows binary from within a Linux WSL environment.

Returns:

The command output logs.

Return type:

Tuple[str, str]

update() → OsirTool[source]

Injects runtime variables into the tool path and command string.

Returns:

The instance with fully resolved and formatted commands.

Return type:

OsirTool

update_env()[source]

Prepares a clean environment variable dictionary for the subprocess.

Returns:

A copy of the environment variables with OSIR-specific additions.

Return type:

dict

src.osir_lib.osir_lib.core.OsirUtils module

src.osir_lib.osir_lib.core.OsirUtils.capture_log_output(target_logger)[source]

Temporarily captures log emissions from a specific logger into an in-memory buffer.

In the OSIR orchestration flow, this context manager is used to intercept module-specific logs (DEBUG, INFO, ERROR) so they can be saved into the task trace database. This provides a granular view of what happened during a specific forensic execution without cluttering the main system log.

Parameters:

target_logger (logging.Logger) – The logger instance to intercept.

Yields:

io.StringIO – A string buffer containing the captured log data.

src.osir_lib.osir_lib.core.OsirUtils.get_latest_log_by_task_id(target_task_id: str, file_path: str = '/OSIR/share/log/task_traces.jsonl')[source]

Retrieves the most recent log trace for a specific Task ID by reading the log file in reverse.

Parameters:

  • target_task_id (str) – The unique identifier of the forensic task.

  • file_path (str) – The path to the JSONL trace file.

Returns:

The parsed JSON trace object if found, None otherwise.

Return type:

dict

src.osir_lib.osir_lib.core.OsirUtils.normalize_osir_path(input_path: str) → str[source]

Standardizes paths to be relative to the framework’s internal share mount point.

Parameters:

input_path (str) – The absolute or prefixed path to normalize.

Returns:

The normalized path starting from /OSIR/share.

Return type:

str

src.osir_lib.osir_lib.core.OsirUtils.remove_placeholders(text: str) → str[source]

Replaces all placeholders {key} with an empty string.

Parameters:

text – The text containing placeholders

Returns:

The text without placeholders
